Circuit        Oescri{3tion
T h e vehicle s p e e d sensor, located on the                            s p e e d o m e t e r      T h e vehicle s p e e d s e n s o r ( V S S ) intermittently           g r o u n d s
d r i v e n g e a r in t h e t r a n s a x l e , g e n e r a t e s p u l s e s i g n a l s t h a t   the circuits.            T h e n u m b e r of p u l s e s per   minute
indicate the vehicle's s p e e d a n d provides signals to the                                       i n c r e a s e / d e c r e a s e with t h e s p e e d of t h e car.
control m o d u l e s to calcaulate the vehicle s p e e d .

Circuit          D e s c r i p t i o n
B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d a t ail t i m e s f r o m t h e p o s i t i v e
battery terminal to the ignition switch a n d the                                 normally
o p e n start relay contacts. W h e n the ignition                               switch
is t u r n e d t o S T A R T a n d t h e t r a n s a x l e r a n g e            switch
( a u t o m a t i c t r a n s a x l e ) is in F V N p o s i t i o n o r t h e s t a r t e r c l u t c h
p e d a l p o s i t i o n s w i t c h ( M a n u a l t r a n s a x l e ) is c l o s e d , b a t t e r y
v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t h r o u g h b u r g l a r a l a r m r e l a y t o t h e
s t a r t e r relay coils. "The start relay coils e n e r g i z e , t h e start
r e l a y c o n t a c t s c l o s e , a n d b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o t h e
starter solenoid. T h e motor e n g a g e s to start the engine.

C i r c u i t     Descri[p't:I<a>ri
"The g e n e r a t o r g e n e r a t e s A C v o l t a g e in its w i n d i n g s              as     W i t h the e n g i n e running a n d the generator charging,
it i s b e l t - d r i v e n b y t h e e n g i n e . T h e r e c t i f i e r c o n v e r t s          t e r m i n a l l_ v o l t a g e r i s e s a n d t h e i n d i c a t o r g o e s o u t . If
this A C voltage to D C                       voltage.                                                t h e g e n e r a t o r f a i l s t o c h a r g e , t e r m i n a l L_ r e m a i n s below
T h e v o l t a g e r e g u l a t o r , a c o m p o n e n t w h i c h is i n c l u d e d              b a t t e r y v o l t a g e a n d t h e i n d i c a t o r r e m a i n s lit.
in t h e g e n e r a t o r f r a m e , h a s a p r i m a r y f u n c t i o n of
controlling the generator's output to m e e t                                  electrical
s y s t e m requirements.                    T h e regulator also              controls
the c h a r g e w a r n i n g              lamp.
Ruse 1T supplies battery voltage to the c h a r g e                                   w a r n i n g
indicator. VVith t h e e n g i n e n o t r u n n i n g a n d t h e                   ignition
s w i t c h i n O N , t e r m i n a l L_ o f t h e r e g u l a t o r i s g r o u n d e d
internally a n d t h e indicator lights up. A s m a l l a m o u n t of
current provided by both the c h a r g e w a r n i n g l a m p a n d the
p r e - e x c i t a t i o n r e s i s t o r is u s e d t o " e x c i t e " t h e  m a g n e t i c
field windings to start the charging                             process.

Circuit       D e s c r i p t i o n
T h e ECIV1 ( E n g i n e C o n t r o l M o d u l e ) c o n t r o l s t h e o p e r a t i o n      T h e m o d u l e also monitors A / C operation through the A / C
of t h e radiator fan m o t o r a n d t h e c o n d e n s e r fan                motor             switch O N input a n d the A / O pressure switch input.             Using
through relay (radiator fan relay a n d c o n d e n s e r fan                                      t h e s e input signals, t h e m o d u l e controls t h e coil of t h e
r e l a y s ) c o n t r o l . T h e ECJVl m o n i t o r s c o o l a n t t e m p e r a t u r e      appropriate relays (condenser fan relays a n d              radiator
through engine coolant temperature                            sensor.                              fan relay) to provide optimal cooling fan operation.

 Circuit       Description
 T h e M u l t i p o r t F u e l I n j e c t i o n ( M F I ) c o n t r o l s y s t e m is a n
 electronic fuel metering system with fuel injectors near
 t h e inlet p o r t of e a c h cylinder. T h e a m o u n t of f u e l i n j e c t i o n
 is d e t e r m i n e d b y t h e E C M a c c o r d i n g t o e n g i n e s p e e d
 a n d i n t a k e air-flow q u a n t i t y m e a s u r e d . "The e m i s s i o n
 control system includes the oxygen sensors and catalytic
 converters. T h e MEI's three major functions are air-fuel
 mixture, idle s p e e d , a n d ignition timing control.                             Refer
 to the s h o p M a n u a l , section E L for details.

 Olrcsuit         Description
 T h e M u l t i p o r t F u e l I n j e c t i o n (IvlF7!) c o n t r o l s y s t e m i s a n
 electronic fuel metering s y s t e m with fuel injectors near
 t h e inlet port of e a c h cylinder. T h e a m o u n t of fuel injection
 i s d e t e r m i n e d b y t h e EEGIvl a c c o r d i n g t o e n g i n e s p e e d
 a n d intake air-flow Quantity m e a s u r e d . T h e emission
 control system includes the o x y g e n sensors a n d catalytic
 c o n v e r t e r s . T h e IvlRI's t h r e e m a j o r f u n c t i o n s a r e a i r - f u e l
 mixture, idle s p e e d , a n d ignition t i m i n g control.                       Refer
 t o t h e s h o p M a n u a l , s e c t i o n Rl_ f o r d e t a i l s .

Circuit Oe^crifptiors
T h e I v l u l t i p o r t F u e l I n j e c t i o n (IVIRI) c o n t r o l s y s t e m i s a n
electronic fuel metering s y s t e m with fuel injectors near
t h e inlet p o r t of e a c h cylinder. T h e a m o u n t of fuel injection
i s d e t e r m i n e d b y t h e EECFvl a c c o r d i n g t o e n g i n e s p e e d
a n d intake air-flow quantity m e a s u r e d . T h e emission
control system includes the oxygen sensors and catalytic
c o n v e r t e r s . T h e IvlFTs t h r e e m a j o r f u n c t i o n s a r e air-fuel
mixture, idle s p e e d , a n d ignition t i m i n g control. R e f e r
to the s h o p M a n u a l , section F L for details.

Circuit       Description
T h e t r a n s a x l e c o n t r o l m o d u l e p r o v i d e s p r e c i s e g e a r shift        T h e t r a n s a x l e c o n t r o l m o d u l e h a s a built-in self d i a g n o s t i c
t i m i n g a n d t o r q u e c o n v e r t e r l o c k - u p by c o n t r o l l i n g t h e         f e a t u r e . Refer to t h e S h o p M a n u a l , s e c t i o n ATlvl, for details.
o p e r a t i o n of t h e a u t o m a t i c t r a n s a x l e s o l e n o i d v a l v e s ( L o w
& reverse v a l v e , d a m p e r c l u t c h c o n t r o l v a l v e , o v e r - d r i v e
valve, 2 n d valve and under-drive solenoid valve).
T h e transaxle control module operates these solenoid
v a l v e s b a s e d o n input s i g n a l s f r o m t h e v a r i o u s s e n s o r s
(for i n s t a n c e , p u l s e g e n e r a t o r # 1 , # 2 ) .

Circuit      D e s c r i p t i o n
T h e transaxle control m o d u l e p r o v i d e s precise g e a r shift                 T h e tra.nsa.xle c o n t r o l m o d u l e h a s a built-in self d i a g n o s t i c
timing a n d torque converter lock-up by controlling the                                  feature. Refer to the S h o p M a n u a l , section A T M , for details.
operation of the a u t o m a t i c t r a n s a x l e s o l e n o i d valves (Low
& reverse valve, d a m p e r clutch control valve, over-drive
valve, 2 n d valve a n d under-drive solenoid valve).
T h e transaxle control m o d u l e operates these solenoid
valves b a s e d o n Input signals f r o m the various s e n s o r s
(for i n s t a n c e , p u l s e g e n e r a t o r # 1 , # 2 ) .

Circuit          D<e*sei-i|3tIon
A B S                                                                                             s i g n a l s , t h e c o n t r o l m o d u l e will d e t e r m i n e t h e       o p t i m u m
"The A n t i - L o o k B r a k e S y s t e m ( A B S ) c o n t r o l s       the                  a m o u n t of w h e e l s p i n . B e c a u s e t h e s y s t e m is v a r i a b l e ,
h y d r a u l i c b r a k e p r e s s u r e of all f o u r w h e e l s    during                  the control unit m a y d e t e r m i n e , d e p e n d i n g o n the driving
s u d d e n braking a n d braking o n h a z a r d o u s                   road                    c o n d i t i o n s , t h a t s o m e w h e e l s p i n is b e n e f i c i a l      (thus
surfaces, preventing the w h e e l s f r o m locking.                          T h e              e n h a n c i n g straight-line acceleration), or that no w h e e l
A B S provides the following                    benefits:                                         s p i n is b e n e f i c i a l ( t h u s e n h a n c i n g c o r n e r i n g ) . F o r a n y
(1 ) E n a b l e s s t e e r i n g a r o u n d o b s t a c l e s w i t h a g r e a t e r          g i v e n driving situation, t h e control m o d u l e will d e t e r m i n e
d e g r e e of c e r t a i n t y d u r i n g p a n i c b r a k i n g .                            the a m o u n t of w h e e l spin best suited to the driver's n e e d s .
(2) E n a b l e s s t o p p i n g d u r i n g p a n i c b r a k i n g w h i l e a l l o w i n g   T h e s y s t e m is a u t o m a t i c a l l y r e a d y w h e n e v e r t h e e n g i n e
stability a n d control e v e n on                curves.                                         is s t a r t e d , b u t c a n b e m a n u a l l y c a n c e l e d w i t h t h e T O S
In c a s e a m a l f u n c t i o n o c c u r s in t h e a n t i - l o c k     brake               switch. T h e T C S provides the following benefits.
s y s t e m , t h e s y s t e m will o p e r a t e a s a n o r m a l          brake               (1 ) W h e n t h e d r i v e w h e e l s p e e d e x c e e d s t h e v e h i c l e
s y s t e m (fail-safe m o d e ) . A d i a g n o s i s function a n d fail-safe                   s p e e d by a given a m o u n t , the A B S control m o d u l e judges
s y s t e m have b e e n included for serviceability.                                             t h a t t h e d r i v e w h e e l s a r e s l i p p i n g , a n d it o u t p u t s t h e
X C S                                                                                             traction control signal to reduce* e n g i n e                          power.
T h e t r a c t i o n c o n t r o l is a v a r i a b l e s y s t e m d e s i g n e d t o          (2) B a s e d o n s i g n a l s f r o m t h e w h e e l s e n s o r s , t h e control
e n h a n c e traction during acceleration a n d cornering.                               It      m o d u l e d e t e c t s a r o u g h roa.dba.sed o n f r e q u e n c y of
d o e s s o by d e t e r m i n i n g t h e o p t i m u m a m o u n t of w h e e l                 w h e e l rotational vibration. T h e control m o d u l e                            signals
spin for a n y given driving situation, t h e n                       suppressing                 t h e T C S c o n t r o l v a l v e a c t u a t o r a n d EEOlVt t o r e l a x e n g i n e
surplus engine p o w e r accordingly. T h e A B S                             control              power, thus improving acceleration efficiency.
m o d u l e gets signals about the vehicle's s p e e d , direction                                 (3) B a s e d o n s i g n a l s a b o u t w h e e l s p e e d , t h e c o n t r o l
a n d road conditions f r o m s e n s o r s at the w h e e l s                      a n d          m o d u l e d e t e r m i n e s t h e efficiency of t h e grip of
the steering c o l u m n .             B a s e d on      these                                    the tires o n the road a n d signals the T C S                                control
                                                                                                   valve actuator a n d the E C M relaxes engine                                   p o w e r
                                                                                                   if n e c e s s a r y , t h u s i m p r o v i n g g r i p .

"The i m m o b i l i z e r c o n t r o l s y s t e m is a n a n t i - t h e f t d e v i c e    T h e t r a n s p o n d e r (toulit-in t h e i g n i t i o n k e y ) s e n d s             unique
w h i c h e n a b l e s t h e s t a r t i n g t o toe p o s s i b l e o n l y   w h e n        frequency secret c o d e signals to the B C M                                   through
the m e c h a n i c a l a n d wireless secret codes,                       m e m o r i z e d   Antenna, coils. T h e BCIvl c o m p a r e s the received                                    c o d e
i n t h e E n g i n e C o n t r o l M o d u l e , a.nd    the B o d y          Control         signals with m e m o r i z e d c o d e . W h e n the c o d e s                              are
ModuIe(BCIVI), are aligned                      simultaneously.                                aligned, the B C M s e n d s signals to the EiCM s o that the
                                                                                               s t a r t i n g is p o s s i b l e t h r o u g h f u e l i n j e c t i o n c o n t r o l .

 Circuit        Description
 Wiper         Operation                                                                W a s h e r      Operation
 B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is s u p p l e d t o t h e w i p e r s w i t c h           B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is s u p p l i e d t o t h e w a s h e r s w i t c h f r o m t h e
 f r o m t h e f u s e 1 O.                                                             f u s e 1 0 . W h e n t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h is t u r n e d o n .
 W h e n t h e ignition s w i t c h is t u r n e d o n . W h e n t h e                  W h e n t h e w a s h e r s w i t c h is in O N , t h e b a t t e r y v o l t a g e
 w i p e r s w i t c h is in L o w o r H i g h p o s i t i o n , t h e b a t t e r y     is g r o u n d e d a t G 1 6 t h r o u g h t h e w a s h e r m o t o r .
 v o l t a g e is g r o u n d e d at G 2 0 ( 2 . 0 L ) or G 2 3 ( 2 . 7 L )
 through the wiper moter.
 Intermittent              Operation
 W h e n t h e w i p e r s w i t c h i n INT" a n d i g n i t i o n s w i t c h is in
 O N , t h e w i p e r r e l a y coil is e n e r g i z e d t h r o u g h BCIvl.
 "The v o l t a g e is g r o u n d e d at G 2 0 ( 2 . 0 L ) o r G 2 3 ( 2 . 7 L )
 t h r o u g h t h e INT" p o s i t i o n o f t h e w i p e r s w i t c h a n d
 t h e l o w position of t h e w i p e r m o t o r .

  Oiroui*          D e s c r i p t i o n
  AM f u n c t i o n s of t h e r e a r w i p e r a n d w a s h e r         circuits      R e a r W a s h e r     O p e r a t i o n
  are controlled by the position of the rear wiper                               a n d    W h e n the W A S H E R           p o s i t i o n is s e l e c t e d , t h e   rear
  w a s h e r s w i t c h in t h e m u l t i f u n c t i o n s w i t c h .                intermittent wiper m o t o r controls the operation                               of
  R o a r W i p e r      O p e r a t i o n                                                t h e w a s h e r m o t o r t h r o u g h a. r e l a y c o i l . T h e       rear
  T h e rear wiper switch h a s 3 positions(OFF, O N                              a n d   wiper operates serveral times after the                               w a s h e r
  INT). B a s e d o n these switch position signals, the                                  fluid sprays onto the rear                    w i n d o w .
  rear intermittent w i p e r m o t o r controls the                       operation
  of t h e w i p e r m o t o r t h r o u g h a relay coil.
  1 . W h e n t h e O N p o s i t i o n is s e l e c t e d , t h e          rear
  w i n d o w wiper operates               continuously.
  2.    W h e n t h e I N T p o s i t i o n is s e l e c t e d , t h e      wiper
  operates         intermittently.

Circuit:      D e s c r i p t i o n
B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d a t all t i m e s t o t h e burglar
a l a r m horn relay f r o m the Ruse                   13.
BCIVI d e t r o t s t h e left d o o r l o c k a c t u a t o r , light    d o o r
lock actuator, tail g a t e u n l o c k s w i t c h , a n d h o o d          switch
a n d s e n d s a s i g n a l to- t h e b u r g l a r a l a r m h o r n    relay
so a s to operate the burglar a l a r m                     horn.

  Gircuit             D e s c r i p t i o n
 T h e o p e r a t i o n of i n d i v i d u a l i n d i c a t o r s is s t a t e d b e l o w .            i n d i c a t o r   B r a k e
  F o r indicators not listed b e l o w (for i n s t a n c e ,                     door                  W h e n t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h in O N o r S T A R T , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e
  ajar, s e a t belt, etc.), s e e t h e s y s t e m i n d i c a t e d                 by                 is a p p l i e d f r o m f u s e 1 7 t o t h e b r a k e w a r n i n g light.              W h e n
 the circuit s c h e m a t i c                 diagrams.                                                 t h e b r a k e is a p p l i e d , t h e s w i t c h c l o s e s a n d p r o v i d e s          a
 Oil P r e s s u r e             I n d i c a t o r                                                       g r o u n d for t h e light. T h e b r a k e w a r n i n g light g o e s o n to
  B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o t h e i n d i c a t o r b u l b f r o m                r e m i n d t h e d r i v e r t h a t t h e p a r k i n g b r a k e is a p p l i e d .
 f u s e 1 ~7 w i t h t h e i g n i t i o n i n O N o r S T A R T . W h e n t h e o i l                   If t h e b r a k e f l u i d l e v e l i s l o w , t h e b r a k e f l u i d l e v e l s w i t c h
 p r e s s u r e is l o w , t h e oil p r e s s u r e s w i t c h c l o s e s , p r o v i d i n g        c l o s e s a n d g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e circuit.                 T h e
 a g r o u n d for t h e i n d i c a t o r bulb. D u e t o t h e oil p r e s s u r e                     b r a k e w a r n i n g light lights to w a r n t h e driver of                        low

 Git-ouif:     Desoriptioi-tt
 B a t t e r y v o l t a g e i s a p p l i e d a t all t i m e s t o t h e d o o r lock          W h e n y o u turn the driver's m a s t e r switch a s s e m b l y t o
 relays a n d u n l o c k relay. W h e n locking t h e v e h i c l e b y u s i n g               the U N L O C K position, a path t o g r o u n d is supplied t o
 t h e d o o r u n l o c k s w i t c h i nt h e left p o w e r w i n d o w s w i t c h , t h e   t h e u n l o c k relay. B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o t h e door
 d o o r u n l o c k s w i t c h will p r o v i d e a g r o u n d t ot h e lock relay            lock actuators. T h e polarity o fthe voltage applied t o the
 a n d the doors lock. W h e n locking the d o o r s b y using the                               actuators is n o w reversed a n d the doors                          unlock.
 v e h i c l e k e y o rp u s h i n g d o w n t h e lock k n o b , a signa.! will b e
 s e n t t o t h e b o d y control m o d u l e . "The c o n t r o l m o d u l e will
 then g r o u n d the relay according t othe vehicle s p e e d                          a n d
 the doors lock. T h e b o d y control m o d u l e also h a s a n input
 f r o m the ignition key switch. This input prevents the d o o r s
 f r o m accidentally being locked w h e n exiting the vehicle
 w i t h t h e ignition off a n d t h e k e y left i n t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h .

Circuit      Desci-iipstion
T h e o p e r a t i o n of t h e t w o o u t s i d e m i r r o r s is c o n t r o l l e d               T h e left/right switch w o r k s similarly to t h e u p / d o w n switch.
 by the p o w e r door mirror switch.                     E a c h mirror      has                       W i t h t h e m a s t e r s e l e c t o r s w i t c h in t h e R I G H T p o s i t i o n ,
two reversible motors: O n e motor m o v e s the                            mirror                      battery voltage a n d g r o u n d a r e applied to the right p o w e r
 u p a n d d o w n , t h e o t h e r m o t o r m o v e s t h e m i r r o r left                         m i r r o r m o t o r s w h i c h o p e r a t e in a s i m i l a r w a y .
 a n d right. T h e m a s t e r s e l e c t o r s w i t c h in t h e       p o w e r
door mirror control switch directs battery                             voltage
to t h e right o r left o u t s i d e         mirror.
W i t h t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h is in A G O o r O N ,         battery
v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d f r o m f u s e 2 3 t o t h e p o w e r d o o r m i r r o r
s w i t c h . W i t h t h e m a s t e r s e l e c t o r s w i t c h in L E F T   a n d
t h e u p / d o w n s w i t c h in U R b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d
to the u p contacts of the u p / d o w n switch a n d to t h e
left p o w e r m i r r o r u p / d o w n m o t o r . G r o u n d is p r o v i d e d
to t h e u p c o n t a c t s of t h e u p / d o w n s w i t c h a n d t h e left
p o w e r mirror u p / d o w n motor a n d the mirror g o e s up.
In t h e D O W N p o s i t i o n , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e a n d g r o u n d      are
a p p l i e d to t h e o p p o s i t e s i d e s of t h e      motor.

 W h e n t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h in O N o r S T A R T a n d             T h e electronic c h r o m e mirror automatically controls
 t h e t r a n s a x l e r a n g e s w i t c h i s i n Ft ( a u t o - m a t i c   t h e glare of t h e h e a d l i g h t s f r o m vehicles b e h i n d
 transaxle)/ back-up switch O N (manual transaxle),                               y o u in l o w - l i g h t s i t u a t i o n s .

 to i m p r o v e visibility w h i l e b a c k i n g up.

Gii-esjit     Des^eriiption
B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is s u p p l i e d a t all t i m e s t o t h e f o l d i n g
a n d unfolding relays from fuse                      13.
"The f o l d i n g a n d u n f o l d i n g relays of B C M c o n t r o l t h e
o p e r a t i o n of t h e o u t s i d e mirror folding m o t o r s (left/right)
by the folding switch.

 Circuit           Description
 /K p e r m a n e n t m a g n e t m o t o r o p e r a t e s e a c h o f t h e p o w e r         W h e n a n y of t h e s w i t c h e s in t h e left p o w e r w i n d o w s w i t c h
 windows. Each motor raises or lowers the glass w h e n                                         a r e o p e r a t e d , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o t h e p o w e r
 v o l t a g e is s u p p l i e d t o it. T h e d i r e c t i o n t h e m o t o r t u r n s     w i n d o w m o t o r . T h e p o w e r w i n d o w m o t o r is g r o u n d e d
 d e p e n d s on the polarity of the s u p p l y voltage.                                      t h r o u g h t h e o p p o s i t e c o n t a c t in t h e left p o w e r w i n d o w
 T h e left p o w e r w i n d o w s w i t c h c o n t r o l s all t h e p o w e r               switch. T h e power w i n d o w motor runs to dirve the window.
 window motors. Each window switch controls only                                                W i n d o w Switch              Operation
 o n e o f t h e p o w e r w i n d o w m o t o r s . If t h e l o c k s w i t c h i s           W i t h the p o w e r w i n d o w relay energized, battery
 d e p r e s s e d , the right p o w e r w i n d o w c a n n o t be controlled                  v o l t a g e is s u p p l i e d t o t h e p a s s e n g e r w i n d o w s w i t c h e s
 b y t h e i r o w n w i n d o w s w i t c h e s , b u t t h e y c a n still b e                a s l o n g a s t h e locVc s w i t c h i n t h e l e f t p o w e r w i n d o w
 c o n t r o l l e d f r o m t h e left p o w e r w i n d o w s w i t c h .                     s w i t c h is O R E ( n o t d e p r e s s e d ) . W h e n t h e p a s s e n g e r
  Left IPower W i n d o w S w i t c h                  Operation                                w i n d o w s s w i t c h is o p e r a t e d , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d
  B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o t h e c o i l a n d t h e c o n t a c t s    to o n e t e r m i n a l of t h e p o w e r w i n d o w m o t o r . T h e o t h e r
 of t h e p o w e r w i n d o w relay at all t i m e s . T h e coil of t h e                    t e r m i n a l is g r o u n d e d t h r o u g h t h e o p p o s i t e c o n t a c t in
  r e l a y is g r o u n d e d t h r o u g h t h e b o d y c o n t r o l m o d u l e            the window switch and the main switch. The power
  (BCIvl) a n y t i m e t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h is O N .                              w i n d o w motor runs to drive the window.
  B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is t h e n s u p p l i e d t o t h e left p o w e r w i n d o w
  s\A/itch t h r o u g h t h e c l o s e d r e l a y c o n t a c t s .

 Circuit         Description
 T h e a u d i o r e c e i v e s b a t t e r y v o l t a g e at all t i m e s f r o m
 fuse 2 to maintain m e m o r y function. Fuse 2 7 supplies
 b a t t e r y v o l t a g e t o t h e a u d i o w h e n t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h is in
 A C C or O N for audio operation a n d d i s p l a y s . T h e a u d i o
 is g r o u n d e d at G 0 5 ( H I G H ) m o d e l / G 1 2 ( L O W ) m o d e l .
 Refer to the s h o p manual, section B E for details

C i r c u i t D^soriipi;ic&n
B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d a t all t i m e s t o b o t h t h e coil
a n d t h e c o n t a c t s of t h e h o r n relay. W i t h t h e h o r n s w i t c h
d e p r e s s e d , g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e c o i l o f t h e h o r n r e l a y
through the horn switch. T h e relay contacts close                                   a n d
apply battery voltage to the horns. S o u n d i n g the horns.

Circuit       Desorijptlora
  L o w B e a m           O p e r a t i o n                                                          F l a s h   O p e r a t i o n
 W i t h t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h in O N , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d    T h e f l a s h f e a t u r e w o r k s w i t h t h e light s w i t c h in all
 to t h e coil of t h e h e a d l a m p relays (low a n d high) f r o m                             positions (OFF, P A R K or H E A D ) . W i t h t h e light switch
 f u s e 2 1 . W i t h t h e light s w i t c h in H E A D a n d t h e                 d i m m e r   in O F F o r P A R K a n d t h e h e a d l a m p d i m m e r s w i t c h in
 s w i t c h in l o w , g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e c o i l o f t h e         low      F L A S H , g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e h i g h h e a d l a m p s          a n d
 h e a d l a m p relays t h r o u g h t h e light s w i t c h . B a t t e r y v o l t a g e         the L o w a n d High h e a d l a m p relays through the                           h e a d
f r o m t h e h e a d l a m p s f u s e is t h e n p r o v i d e d t o t h e l o w                  l a m p d i m m e r / p a s s i n g s w i t c h . B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d
 b e a m h e a d l a m p s through the c l o s e d contacts of t h e h e a d                        to the high b e a m h e a d l a m p a n d the b e a m                        indicator,
 l a m p relay (low). T h e l o w b e a m h e a d l a m p s light.                                  sand t h e h i g h b e a m s a n d t h e h i g h b e a m i n d i c a t o r l i g h t a s
 H i g h B e a m           O p e r a t i o n                                                        l o n g a s t h e s w i t c h is h e l d in t h e F L A S H           position.
W i t h t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h in O N , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d
to t h e coil of h e a d l a m p relays (low a n d high) t h r o u g h f u s e
2 1 . W i t h t h e light s w i t c h in H E A D a n d t h e d i m m e r s w i t c h
 in h i g h , g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e c o i l o f t h e h i g h         h e a d
l a m p relay t h r o u g h t h e light s w i t c h . B a t t e r y v o l t a g e f r o m
t h e h e a d l a m p f u s e is t h e n p r o v i d e d t o t h e h i g h             b e a m
h e a d l a m p s t h r o u g h t h e c l o s e d c o n t a c t s of the             h e a d
lamp relay (high). With the h e a d l a m p                           d i m m e r / p a s s i n g
s w i t c h in H I G H , g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e h i g h            b e a m
h e a d l a m p s through the h e a d l a m p                    d i m m e r / p a s s i n g
switch.        B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a l s o p r o v i d e d t o t h e h i g h
b e a m i n d i c a t o r in t h e i n s t r u m e n t     cluster.

Circuit        Description
For t h e visibility a n d safty of drivers, t h e d a y t i m e r u n n i n g                 In a d d i t i o n t o t h e D R L r e l a y c o n t r o l , t h e D R L c o n t r o l m o d -
l i g h t s will t u r n o n a u t o m a t i c a l l y w h e n t h e e n g i n e is r u n -   ule c o n t r o l s t h e tail relay (built in to t h e D R L c o n t r o l m o d u l e )
ning. U n d e r this condition, t h e h e a d l a m p s will illuminate                       c o i l . B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o t h e c l o s e d c o n t a c t of t h e
with a minimal a m o u n t of load o n t h e c h a r g i n g s y s t e m .                    tail l a m p relay a n d t h e interior illumination l a m p s t u r n o n .
A s the D H L control module detects the generator                                   run-     W h e n the D R L module detects the engine running                                          O F F
ning signals (engine running signal), the D H L                                  control      s i g n a l , t h e D R L c o n t r o l m o d u l e t u r n s t h e l i g h t s off.       W h e n
m o d u l e c o n t r o l s t h e o p e r a t i o n of t h e h i g h b e a m        head      t h e D R L m o d u l e d e t e c t s t h e l i g h t s w i t c h OISI ( P A R K o r
lamps through              D R L     relay coil control.               Battery      volt-     H E A D ) s i g n a l , t h e D R L c o n t r o l m o d u l e s t o p s it's f u n c t i o n s .
a g e f r o m F u s i b l e l i n k ( B A 1 T) i s t h e n p r o v i d e d t o        the
low b e a m h e a d lamps through the D R L relay contact.

 Circuit          Description
 F o r t h e visibility of driver, t h e a u t o light s e n s o r c o n t r o l s                     A u t o light s e n s o r s e n d s o u t p u t signals to t h e B C M
 a u t o m a t i c a l l y t h e h e a d l a m p s a n d tail l a m p s t h r o u g h relay            a n d t h e B O M c o n t r o l s t h e tail l a m p relay.
 coil c o n t r o l . W h e n t h e light s w i t c h in A U T O a n d t h e a u t o                   O f c o u r s e , t h e h e a d l a m p s a n d tail l a m p s c a n
 light s e n s o r d e t e c t s l o w i n t e n s i t y of i l l u m i n a t i o n , g r o u n d is   b e controlled i n d e p e n d e n t l y b y u s i n g light switch
 p r o v i d e d to t h e h e a d l a m p relays t h r o u g h a u t o light sensor-                   and dimmer/passing switch.
 B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is s u p p l i e d t o t h e h e a d l a m p s t h r o u g h
 head lamp fuses a n d h e a d lamp relays(high/ low) contact.

C i r c u i t      Dosorljption
"The h e a d l a m p s s h o u l d b e a i m e d p r o p e r l y a c c o r d i n g   to   W h e n     the       light s w i t c h is in t h e     h e a d  position      battery
the n u m b e r of the p a s s e n g e r s a n d the loading w e i g h t             in   voltage        is s u p p l i e d t o t h e   left/right h e a d    l a m p   leveling
the luggages area.          To adjust the h e a d l a m p s b e a m             level,    actuators            through       the   h e a d   l a m p    relay(low)      contact
turn the h e a d l a m p leveling switch. T h e higher the                      n u m -   a n d   the      b e a m    level c a n     b e controlled through            a  vari-
ber of the switch position, the lower t h e h e a d l a m p                    b e a m    a b l e r e s i s t o r (bulit in t o t h e h e a d l a m p l e v e l i n g   switch).
level. A l w a y s k e e p the h e a d l a m p s b e a m at t h e p r o p e r lev-
eling position, or h e a d l a m p m a y dazzle other road users.

 Circuit      D e s c r i p t i o n
B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d a t all t i m e s t o t h e c o i l a n d
c o n t a c t s o f t h e t a i l l a m p r e l a y f r o m t h e B A T T f u s i b l e link:.
W i t h t h e light s w i t c h in t h e m u l t i f u n c t i o n s w i t o h in
P A R K o r H E A D p o s i t i o n , t h e coil of t h e tail l a m p relay
is g r o u n d e d t h r o u g h t h e light s w i t c h . B a t t e r y v o l t a g e
f r o m f u s e 9 a n d f u s e 1 4 is t h e n p r o v i d e d t o t h e          l a m p s
(Right/Left s i d e m a r k e r l a m p s , L e f t / R i g h t tail l a m p s ,
Left/Right park l a m p a n d license lamps) through the
tail l a m p r e l a y c o n t a c t a n d all t h e l a m p s g o o n .

Circuit        Desorljptiora
W i t h t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h in O N I o r S T A R T , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is
applied f r o m fuse 3 to the back-up l a m p s switch                                   ( m a n u a l
transaxle) or the transaxle range switch                                  (automatic
t r a n s a x l e ) . W i t h t h e t r a n s a x l e in r e v e r s e , b a t t e r y v o l t a g e is
a p p l i e d t o t h e b a c k - u p l a m p s a n d t h e b a c k - u p l a m p s go              on.

 B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o t h e s t o p l a m p s w i t c h a t all

Circuit       D e s c r i p t i o n
B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d a t all t i m e s t o t h e c o i l a n d     B a t t e r y v o l t a g e f r o m f u s e 9 a n d f u s e 1 4 is t h e n     provided
c o n t a c t s of t h e tail l a m p relay f r o m t h e B A T T f u s i b l e            to t h e illumination l a m p s t h r o u g h t h e c l o s e d c o n t a c t of
link. W i t h t h e light s w i t c h ( m u l t i f u n c t i o n s w i t c h ) in t h e   t h e tail l a m p relay. G r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e i l l u m i n a t i o n
R A R K o r H E A D p o s i t i o n , g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e tail        l a m p s through the rheostat.                 "The r h e o s t a t c o n t r o l s
l a m p relay coil t h r o u g h t h e light s w i t c h .                                 brightness of the illumination                     lamps.

 Circuit       Description
 Only authorized service personnel should do work on                                               W i t h t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h in O N o r START", b a t t e r y v o l t a g e
 or a r o u n d the supplemental restraint s y s t e m (SRS)                                       is a p p l i e d t o t h e S R S c o n t r o l m o d u l e f r o m f u s e 5 t o t h e
 components. Service personnel should read section                                                 S R S i n d i c a t o r in t h e i n s t r u m e n t c l u s t e r f r o m f u s e 4 . W h e i
 5 6 a of the service m a n u a l carefully before starting w o r k                                t h e i g n i t i o n s w i t c h is t u r n e d O N , t h e S R S i n d i c a t o r in
 on the supplemental restraint s y s t e m (SRS).                                                  t h e i n s t r u m e n t c l u s t e r w i l l f l a s h s i x ; t i m e s . If t h e S R S
 "The s u p p l e m e n t a l r e s t r a i n t s y s t e m ( S R S ) is a s a f e t y             i n d i c a t o r d o e s n o t f l a s h six; t i m e s , s t a y s o n o r c o m e s o n
 d e v i c e w h i c h w h e n u s e d in c o n j u n c t i o n w i t h t h e s e a t belts        w h i l e d r i v i n g , t h e S R S is n o t w o r k i n g p r o p e r l y .
 is d e s i g n e d to p r o t e c t t h e d r i v e r a n d f r o n t s e a t p a s s e n g e r
 b y d e p l o y i n g t h e air b a g s w h e n t h e S R S C M              receives
 a frontal i m p a c t e x c e e d i n g a c e r t a i n s e t limit.

 Circuit      D e s c r i p t i o n
 T h e cruise control s y s t e m uses m e c h a n i c a l a n d              With cruise e n g a g e d , the cruise control m o d u l e              s e n d s
 electrical devices to maintain vehicle s p e e d          at                 a signal to the transaxle control m o d u l e that prevents
 a driver selected setting.                                                   the transaxle f r o m continuously downshifting                     a n d
 T h e cruise control m o d u l e receives signals from the cruise            upshifting to maintain the set vehicle s p e e d w h e n                    going
 control m a i n switch, the stop l a m p switch, the cruise                  u p a l o n g g r a d e or hill. T h e c r u i s e c o n t r o l m o d u l e
 control switch, transaxle range switch (A/T), cruise clutch                  incorporates a self diagnostic feature.
 pedal position switch (M/T) a n d the vehicle s p e e d sensor.
 T h e cruise control m o d u l e s e n d s signals to the cruise
 control actuator a n d the transaxle control module(A/T).

position except O F F , the blower relay coil e n e r g i z e s                           a n d      a n y position e x c e p t OEE, the blower relay coil energizes
t h e b l o w e r r e l a y c o n t a c t c l o s e s . B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is t h e n       a n d the blower relay contact closes. Battery voltage
applied to the blower m o t o r t h r o u g h the c l o s e d contacts of                            is t h e n a p p l i e d t o t h e b l o w e r m o t o r t h r o u g h t h e      closed
t h e b l o w e r r e l a y a n d t h e n g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d t o t h e b l o w e r       c o n t a c t s o f t h e b l o w e r r e l a y a n d t h e n g r o u n d is p r o v i d e d
m o t o r through the blower resistor a n d the blower switch.                                       to the blower m o t o r through the blower resistor a n d the
A s t h e b l o w e r s w i t c h is m o v e d f r o m O E E t o H I ,             resistance        b l o w e r s w i t c h . B a t t e r y v o l t a g e is a p p l i e d t o A / C   switch
of t h e resistors (built in to t h e b l o w e r resistor) will                                     t h r o u g h t h e f u s e 1 5 . W i t h t h e A / C s w i t c h in O N I , t h e
d e c r e a s e a n d battery voltage applied to the blower                              m o t o r   E n g i n e C o n t r o l M o d u l e (EOlvl) will g r o u n d the coil of the
will i n c r e a s e . T h i s will i n c r e a s e t h e b l o w e r m o t o r s p e e d .          A / C control relay after a s h o r t delay to adjust idle s p e e d .
W h e n t h e b l o w e r s w i t c h is in H I , all of t h e r e s i s t o r s         are         B a t t e r y v o l t a g e will t h e n b e a p p l i e d to t h e       c o m p r e s s o r
b y p a s s e d a n d t h e m a x i m u m v o l t a g e is n o w a p p l i e d t o t h e             m a g n e t i c clutch f r o m the A / C fuse through the closed
b l o w e r m o t o r s o t h a t it r u n s a t t h e h i g h e s t s p e e d .                     c o n t a c t s of t h e A / C relay. T h e A / C c o m p r e s s o r              runs.
